title: Japan's Defense Minister Koizumi Plans June Visit to South Korea for Talks on North Korean Missile Threats and Defense Cooperation
summary: Japanese Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi announced plans to visit South Korea in late June for defense talks addressing North Korea's ongoing nuclear and missile development. The meeting with South Korean counterpart Ahn Gyu-back will focus on deepening bilateral security cooperation amid DPRK ballistic missile launches threatening both nations. Koizumi may also visit the Panmunjom border area.
contents:
# Japan's Defense Minister Koizumi Plans June Visit to South Korea for Talks on North Korean Missile Threats and Defense Cooperation

## Overview
On April 30, 2026, Japanese Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi revealed his intention to travel to South Korea in late June for his first official visit since taking office in October 2025, aiming to hold high-level talks with Defense Minister Ahn Gyu-back. This follows Ahn's February 2026 visit to Japan, where both sides affirmed plans to enhance national security cooperation through reciprocal visits and joint efforts. Key discussion points include bolstering interactions between Japan's Self-Defense Forces and South Korean military units, as well as coordinating responses to North Korea's persistent nuclear and missile programs, which have seen repeated ballistic missile tests posing direct threats to Japan and South Korea. Sources indicate Koizumi remains considering a trip to the Joint Security Area at Panmunjom on the inter-Korean border to underscore the urgency of regional stability. This development occurs against a backdrop of stalled Japan-North Korea relations, with Tokyo's dialogue attempts rebuffed since a failed 2024 contact in Mongolia, and amid broader concerns over DPRK's advancing capabilities highlighted in Japan's 2025 Defense White Paper. The talks reflect Japan's commitment to countering the DPRK missile threat through strengthened alliances, potentially integrating missile defense strategies in the face of growing Russia-North Korea military ties.

## Location
- **Where**: Tokyo, Japan
- **Country**: 日本

## Source Attribution
